column1: # When automatic matching (callsign / normalized-name / token-set) can't
# pin upstream EPG data to one of your M3U channels, add a line here:
# Example: if your M3U has a channel with tvg-id `BBC.One.Local.uk` and the
# upstream iptv-org/epg source emits programmes against id `bbc1.uk@LDN`,
# the line below routes upstream data to your channel:
#   * One alias per line. Comments start with '#'.
#   * The fields are TAB-separated (single tab character).
#   * Upstream id is matched against the channel-id of any provider /
#   * Aliases are applied during the backfill pass — they take priority
#     over automatic callsign / name-based matching, so use sparingly to
#     fix hard-to-pin cases (Arabic-script channels, region splits, etc.).
